# give-claude-code-voice-tts

> A Claude Code plugin that gives Claude Code a spoken voice on Windows: a Stop hook + a persistent
> edge-tts daemon read a short <spoken> summary of each reply aloud, with a skill to switch voices,
> mute, and interrupt. Off by default, opt-in per project.

## Canonical
- GitHub: https://github.com/MarioMagdy/give-claude-code-voice-tts

## What it is
- A Claude Code plugin: SessionStart/Stop/SessionEnd hooks + a warm edge-tts daemon (`hooks/daemon.py`)
  + the `speech` control skill (`skills/speech/SKILL.md`).
- Claude ends conversational replies with `<spoken>...</spoken>`; the hook speaks just that summary
  (never code/tables). Free neural TTS via Microsoft Edge (`edge-tts`); no cloud account.

## Install
- `/plugin marketplace add MarioMagdy/give-claude-code-voice-tts`
- `/plugin install voice-tts@give-claude-code-voice-tts`
- Turn it on for a project: `/speech` then enable (off by default everywhere).

## Key docs
- [README.md](README.md): how it works, install, voices, FAQ, requirements.
- [skills/speech/SKILL.md](skills/speech/SKILL.md): voice/mute/interrupt control surface.
- [CONTINGENCIES.md](CONTINGENCIES.md): pre-decided fallbacks for known failure modes.

## Use it when
You want Claude Code to talk — read its replies aloud while you keep working — on Windows. Trigger
phrases: "make Claude talk", "turn on TTS", "give Claude a voice", "stop speaking", "change the voice",
"switch voice", "speak again", "mute", "unmute", "interrupt the audio", "stop talking right now",
"read your replies aloud", "calmer voice", "more British voice", "male voice", "expressive voice",
"text-to-speech for Claude Code", "Claude Code accessibility".

## Not for
- macOS (playback uses Windows MCI via `winmm.dll`).
- Linux (same — playback is Windows-only).
- Anyone who wants every reply read aloud, including code/tables (data-shaped replies are
  deliberately silent; only `<spoken>` summaries are spoken).
- Servers / headless Linux boxes without a desktop audio stack.

## Free
- Uses Microsoft Edge neural voices via `edge-tts` — free, no account, no API key.
- No Google, Azure, or AWS TTS billing.
- The optional `keyboard` library (for the `Ctrl+Alt+S` interrupt hotkey) is a free PyPI package.
